tEn: What Uncertainties Do We Need in Bayesian Deep Learning for Computer Vision?
tZh: 贝叶斯深度学习在计算机视觉中需要哪些不确定性？
sEn: Two types of uncertainty, aleatoric and epistemic, are modeled in Bayesian deep learning for computer vision, with aleatoric capturing observation noise and epistemic capturing model uncertainty that can be reduced with more data.
sZh: 贝叶斯深度学习在计算机视觉中建模两种不确定性：偶然不确定性捕捉观测噪声，认知不确定性捕捉模型不确定性，后者可通过更多数据消除。
absEn: There are two major types of uncertainty one can model. Aleatoric uncertainty captures noise inherent in the observations. On the other hand, epistemic uncertainty accounts for uncertainty in the model -- uncertainty which can be explained away given enough data. Traditionally it has been difficult to model epistemic uncertainty in computer vision, but with new Bayesian deep learning tools this is now possible. We study the benefits of modeling epistemic vs. aleatoric uncertainty in Bayesian deep learning models for vision tasks. For this we present a Bayesian deep learning framework combining input-dependent aleatoric uncertainty together with epistemic uncertainty. We study models under the framework with per-pixel semantic segmentation and depth regression tasks. Further, our explicit uncertainty formulation leads to new loss functions for these tasks, which can be interpreted as learned attenuation. This makes the loss more robust to noisy data, also giving new state-of-the-art results on segmentation and depth regression benchmarks.
absZh: 存在两种主要的不确定性类型可以建模。偶然不确定性捕捉观测中固有的噪声。另一方面，认知不确定性解释模型中的不确定性——这种不确定性在给定足够数据的情况下可以被解释掉。传统上，在计算机视觉中建模认知不确定性一直很困难，但借助新的贝叶斯深度学习工具，现在这已成为可能。我们研究了在视觉任务的贝叶斯深度学习模型中建模认知不确定性与偶然不确定性的益处。为此，我们提出了一个结合输入相关偶然不确定性和认知不确定性的贝叶斯深度学习框架。我们研究了该框架下逐像素语义分割和深度回归任务的模型。此外，我们明确的不确定性公式为这些任务带来了新的损失函数，这些损失函数可以解释为学习到的衰减。这使得损失对噪声数据更加鲁棒，也在分割和深度回归基准上给出了新的最先进结果。

contrib.en: Proposes a unified Bayesian deep learning framework combining input-dependent aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty.
contrib.zh: 提出一个统一的贝叶斯深度学习框架，结合了输入依赖的偶然不确定性和认知不确定性。

contrib.en: Derives new loss functions for regression and classification that act as learned attenuation, improving robustness to noisy data.
contrib.zh: 推导出回归和分类的新损失函数，作为学习衰减，提高对噪声数据的鲁棒性。

contrib.en: Achieves state-of-the-art results on semantic segmentation (CamVid, NYUv2) and depth regression (Make3D, NYUv2 Depth) benchmarks.
contrib.zh: 在语义分割（CamVid、NYUv2）和深度回归（Make3D、NYUv2 Depth）基准上取得最先进结果。

contrib.en: Shows aleatoric uncertainty captures inherent noise and improves performance in large-data regimes, while epistemic uncertainty detects out-of-distribution examples.
contrib.zh: 表明偶然不确定性捕捉固有噪声并在大数据场景下提升性能，而认知不确定性检测分布外样本。

contrib.en: Demonstrates that combining both uncertainties outperforms modeling either alone, with minimal added compute for aleatoric uncertainty.
contrib.zh: 证明结合两种不确定性优于单独建模，且偶然不确定性几乎不增加计算量。

limits.en: Epistemic uncertainty via Monte Carlo dropout is computationally expensive, causing up to 50x slowdown for 50 samples.
limits.zh: 通过蒙特卡洛丢弃法建模认知不确定性计算成本高，50个样本导致高达50倍减速。

limits.en: Aleatoric uncertainty alone cannot detect out-of-distribution examples, limiting its use in safety-critical applications.
limits.zh: 仅靠偶然不确定性无法检测分布外样本，限制了其在安全关键应用中的使用。

limits.en: The framework assumes Gaussian or Laplacian likelihoods, which may not be optimal for all vision tasks.
limits.zh: 该框架假设高斯或拉普拉斯似然，可能不适用于所有视觉任务。

limits.en: Evaluation is limited to semantic segmentation and depth regression; applicability to other vision tasks (e.g., object detection) is not explored.
limits.zh: 评估仅限于语义分割和深度回归，未探索对其他视觉任务（如目标检测）的适用性。

limits.en: The method requires careful tuning of dropout probability and Monte Carlo sample count for optimal performance.
limits.zh: 该方法需要仔细调整丢弃概率和蒙特卡洛样本数量以达到最佳性能。

paras.en: There are two major types of uncertainty one can model. Aleatoric uncertainty captures noise inherent in the observations. On the other hand, epistemic uncertainty accounts for uncertainty in the model – uncertainty which can be explained away given enough data. Traditionally it has been difficult to model epistemic uncertainty in computer vision, but with new Bayesian deep learning tools this is now possible. We study the benefits of modeling epistemic vs. aleatoric uncertainty in Bayesian deep learning models for vision tasks. For this we present a Bayesian deep learning framework combining input-dependent aleatoric uncertainty together with epistemic uncertainty. We study models under the framework with per-pixel semantic segmentation and depth regression tasks. Further, our explicit uncertainty formulation leads to new loss functions for these tasks, which can be interpreted as learned attenuation. This makes the loss more robust to noisy data, also giving new state-of-the-art results on segmentation and depth regression benchmarks.
paras.zh: 可以建模两种主要的不确定性类型。偶然不确定性捕捉观测中固有的噪声。另一方面，认知不确定性解释模型中的不确定性——这种不确定性在给定足够数据的情况下可以被解释消除。传统上，在计算机视觉中建模认知不确定性是困难的，但借助新的贝叶斯深度学习工具，现在这已成为可能。我们研究了在视觉任务的贝叶斯深度学习模型中建模认知不确定性与偶然不确定性的益处。为此，我们提出了一个结合输入相关偶然不确定性和认知不确定性的贝叶斯深度学习框架。我们研究了该框架下逐像素语义分割和深度回归任务的模型。此外，我们明确的不确定性公式为这些任务带来了新的损失函数，这些损失函数可以解释为学习到的衰减。这使得损失对噪声数据更加鲁棒，也在分割和深度回归基准上取得了新的最先进结果。

paras.en: Understanding what a model does not know is a critical part of many machine learning systems. Today, deep learning algorithms are able to learn powerful representations which can map high dimensional data to an array of outputs. However these mappings are often taken blindly and assumed to be accurate, which is not always the case. In two recent examples this has had disastrous consequences. In May 2016 there was the first fatality from an assisted driving system, caused by the perception system confusing the white side of a trailer for bright sky [1]. In a second recent example, an image classification system erroneously identified two African Americans as gorillas [2], raising concerns of racial discrimination. If both these algorithms were able to assign a high level of uncertainty to their erroneous predictions, then the system may have been able to make better decisions and likely avoid disaster.
paras.zh: 理解模型不知道什么是许多机器学习系统的关键部分。如今，深度学习算法能够学习强大的表示，将高维数据映射到一系列输出。然而，这些映射通常被盲目接受并假定为准确，但事实并非总是如此。最近的两个例子导致了灾难性后果。2016年5月，辅助驾驶系统首次发生致命事故，原因是感知系统将拖车的白色侧面误认为是明亮的天空[1]。在另一个最近的例子中，图像分类系统错误地将两名非裔美国人识别为大猩猩[2]，引发了种族歧视的担忧。如果这两个算法都能为其错误预测分配高不确定性，那么系统可能能够做出更好的决策，并可能避免灾难。

paras.en: Quantifying uncertainty in computer vision applications can be largely divided into regression settings such as depth regression, and classification settings such as semantic segmentation. Existing approaches to model uncertainty in such settings in computer vision include particle filtering and conditional random fields [3, 4]. However many modern applications mandate the use of deep learning to achieve state-of-the-art performance [5], with most deep learning models not able to represent uncertainty. Deep learning does not allow for uncertainty representation in regression settings for example, and deep learning classification models often give normalised score vectors, which do not necessarily capture model uncertainty. For both settings uncertainty can be captured with Bayesian deep learning approaches – which offer a practical framework for understanding uncertainty with deep learning models [6].
paras.zh: 在计算机视觉应用中量化不确定性主要可分为回归设置（如深度回归）和分类设置（如语义分割）。在计算机视觉中，现有建模不确定性的方法包括粒子滤波和条件随机场[3, 4]。然而，许多现代应用要求使用深度学习以达到最先进的性能[5]，而大多数深度学习模型无法表示不确定性。例如，深度学习在回归设置中不允许不确定性表示，而深度学习分类模型通常给出归一化的得分向量，这不一定能捕捉模型不确定性。对于这两种设置，不确定性可以通过贝叶斯深度学习方法来捕捉——这为理解深度学习模型的不确定性提供了一个实用框架[6]。

paras.en: In Bayesian modeling, there are two main types of uncertainty one can model [7]. Aleatoric uncertainty captures noise inherent in the observations. This could be for example sensor noise or motion noise, resulting in uncertainty which cannot be reduced even if more data were to be collected. On the other hand, epistemic uncertainty accounts for uncertainty in the model parameters – uncertainty which captures our ignorance about which model generated our collected data. This uncertainty can be explained away given enough data, and is often referred to as model uncertainty. Aleatoric uncertainty can further be categorized into homoscedastic uncertainty, uncertainty which stays constant for different inputs, and heteroscedastic uncertainty. Heteroscedastic uncertainty depends on the inputs to the model, with some inputs potentially having more noisy outputs than others. Heteroscedastic uncertainty is especially important for computer vision applications. For example, for depth regression, highly textured input images with strong vanishing lines are expected to result in confident predictions, whereas an input image of a featureless wall is expected to have very high uncertainty.
paras.zh: 在贝叶斯建模中，可以建模两种主要类型的不确定性[7]。偶然不确定性捕捉观测中固有的噪声。这可能是传感器噪声或运动噪声，导致即使收集更多数据也无法减少的不确定性。另一方面，认知不确定性解释了模型参数的不确定性——这种不确定性捕捉了我们对于哪个模型生成了收集到的数据的无知。这种不确定性在给定足够数据的情况下可以被解释，通常被称为模型不确定性。偶然不确定性可以进一步分为同方差不确定性（对于不同输入保持不变的不确定性）和异方差不确定性。异方差不确定性取决于模型的输入，某些输入可能比其他输入有更多噪声的输出。异方差不确定性对于计算机视觉应用尤其重要。例如，对于深度回归，具有强烈消失线的高度纹理输入图像预计会产生自信的预测，而特征缺失的墙壁输入图像预计会有非常高的不确定性。

paras.en: In this paper we make the observation that in many big data regimes (such as the ones common to deep learning with image data), it is most effective to model aleatoric uncertainty, uncertainty which cannot be explained away. This is in comparison to epistemic uncertainty which is mostly explained away with the large amounts of data often available in machine vision. We further show that modeling aleatoric uncertainty alone comes at a cost. Out-of-data examples, which can be identified with epistemic uncertainty, cannot be identified with aleatoric uncertainty alone.
paras.zh: 在本文中，我们观察到在许多大数据场景中（例如深度学习处理图像数据时常见的场景），建模偶然不确定性（无法被解释的不确定性）最为有效。这与认知不确定性形成对比，后者在机器视觉中通常可用的大量数据下大多被解释。我们进一步表明，仅建模偶然不确定性是有代价的。可以通过认知不确定性识别的分布外示例，无法仅通过偶然不确定性识别。

paras.en: For this we present a unified Bayesian deep learning framework which allows us to learn mappings from input data to aleatoric uncertainty and compose these together with epistemic uncertainty approximations. We derive our framework for both regression and classification applications and present results for per-pixel depth regression and semantic segmentation tasks (see Figure 1 and the supplementary video for examples). We show how modeling aleatoric uncertainty in regression can be used to learn loss attenuation, and develop a complimentary approach for the classification case. This demonstrates the efficacy of our approach on difficult and large scale tasks.
paras.zh: 为此，我们提出了一个统一的贝叶斯深度学习框架，允许我们学习从输入数据到偶然不确定性的映射，并将其与认知不确定性近似相结合。我们为回归和分类应用推导了我们的框架，并展示了逐像素深度回归和语义分割任务的结果（参见图1和补充视频中的示例）。我们展示了如何在回归中建模偶然不确定性以学习损失衰减，并为分类情况开发了一种补充方法。这证明了我们的方法在困难和大规模任务上的有效性。

paras.en: We capture an accurate understanding of aleatoric and epistemic uncertainties, in particular with a novel approach for classification,
paras.zh: 我们通过一种新颖的分类方法，准确捕捉了偶然不确定性和认知不确定性，

paras.en: We improve model performance by 1−3%1percent31-3\% over non-Bayesian baselines by reducing the effect of noisy data with the implied attenuation obtained from explicitly representing aleatoric uncertainty,
paras.zh: 通过显式表示偶然不确定性获得的隐含衰减减少噪声数据的影响，我们将模型性能比非贝叶斯基线提高了1-3%，

paras.en: We study the trade-offs between modeling aleatoric or epistemic uncertainty by characterizing the properties of each uncertainty and comparing model performance and inference time.
paras.zh: 我们通过表征每种不确定性的属性并比较模型性能和推理时间，研究了建模偶然不确定性或认知不确定性之间的权衡。

paras.en: Existing approaches to Bayesian deep learning capture either epistemic uncertainty alone, or aleatoric uncertainty alone [6]. These uncertainties are formalised as probability distributions over either the model parameters, or model outputs, respectively. Epistemic uncertainty is modeled by placing a prior distribution over a model’s weights, and then trying to capture how much these weights vary given some data. Aleatoric uncertainty on the other hand is modeled by placing a distribution over the output of the model. For example, in regression our outputs might be modeled as corrupted with Gaussian random noise. In this case we are interested in learning the noise’s variance as a function of different inputs (such noise can also be modeled with a constant value for all data points, but this is of less practical interest). These uncertainties, in the context of Bayesian deep learning, are explained in more detail in this section.
paras.zh: 现有的贝叶斯深度学习方法要么仅捕获认知不确定性，要么仅捕获偶然不确定性[6]。这些不确定性分别被形式化为模型参数或模型输出的概率分布。认知不确定性通过在模型权重上放置先验分布，然后尝试捕捉这些权重在给定数据下的变化程度来建模。而偶然不确定性则通过在模型输出上放置分布来建模。例如，在回归中，我们的输出可能被建模为被高斯随机噪声污染。在这种情况下，我们感兴趣的是学习噪声方差作为不同输入的函数（这种噪声也可以用所有数据点的常数值来建模，但这实际意义较小）。这些不确定性在贝叶斯深度学习背景下的详细解释将在本节中给出。

full.sec: 2.1 Epistemic Uncertainty in Bayesian Deep Learning
full.secZh: 2.1 贝叶斯深度学习中的认知不确定性

paras.en: To capture epistemic uncertainty in a neural network (NN) we put a prior distribution over its weights, for example a Gaussian prior distribution: 𝐖∼𝒩​(0,I)similar-to𝐖𝒩0𝐼\mathbf{W}\sim\mathcal{N}(0,I).
paras.zh: 为了捕捉神经网络（NN）中的认知不确定性，我们对其权重设置一个先验分布，例如高斯先验分布：𝐖∼𝒩(0,I)。

paras.en: Such a model is referred to as a Bayesian neural network (BNN) [9, 10, 11]. Bayesian neural networks replace the deterministic network’s weight parameters with distributions over these parameters, and instead of optimising the network weights directly we average over all possible weights (referred to as marginalisation). Denoting the random output of the BNN as 𝐟𝐖​(𝐱)superscript𝐟𝐖𝐱\mathbf{f}^{\mathbf{W}}(\mathbf{x}), we define the model likelihood p​(𝐲|𝐟𝐖​(𝐱))𝑝conditional𝐲superscript𝐟𝐖𝐱p(\mathbf{y}|\mathbf{f}^{\mathbf{W}}(\mathbf{x})). Given a dataset 𝐗={𝐱1,…,𝐱N},𝐘={𝐲1,…,𝐲N}formulae-sequence𝐗subscript𝐱1…subscript𝐱𝑁𝐘subscript𝐲1…subscript𝐲𝑁\mathbf{X}=\{\mathbf{x}_{1},...,\mathbf{x}_{N}\},\mathbf{Y}=\{\mathbf{y}_{1},...,\mathbf{y}_{N}\}, Bayesian inference is used to compute the posterior over the weights p​(𝐖|𝐗,𝐘)𝑝conditional𝐖𝐗𝐘p(\mathbf{W}|\mathbf{X},\mathbf{Y}). This posterior captures the set of plausible model parameters, given the data.
paras.zh: 这样的模型被称为贝叶斯神经网络（BNN）[9, 10, 11]。贝叶斯神经网络将确定性网络的权重参数替换为这些参数上的分布，并且不是直接优化网络权重，而是对所有可能的权重进行平均（称为边缘化）。将BNN的随机输出记为𝐟^𝐖(𝐱)，我们定义模型似然p(𝐲|𝐟^𝐖(𝐱))。给定数据集𝐗={𝐱₁,…,𝐱_N}, 𝐘={𝐲₁,…,𝐲_N}，使用贝叶斯推理计算权重的后验分布p(𝐖|𝐗,𝐘)。该后验分布捕捉了给定数据下合理的模型参数集合。

paras.en: For regression tasks we often define our likelihood as a Gaussian with mean given by the model output: p​(𝐲|𝐟𝐖​(𝐱))=𝒩​(𝐟𝐖​(𝐱),σ2)𝑝conditional𝐲superscript𝐟𝐖𝐱𝒩superscript𝐟𝐖𝐱superscript𝜎2p(\mathbf{y}|\mathbf{f}^{\mathbf{W}}(\mathbf{x}))=\mathcal{N}(\mathbf{f}^{\mathbf{W}}(\mathbf{x}),\sigma^{2}), with an observation noise scalar σ𝜎\sigma. For classification, on the other hand, we often squash the model output through a softmax function, and sample from the resulting probability vector: p​(𝐲|𝐟𝐖​(𝐱))=Softmax​(𝐟𝐖​(𝐱))𝑝conditional𝐲superscript𝐟𝐖𝐱Softmaxsuperscript𝐟𝐖𝐱p(\mathbf{y}|\mathbf{f}^{\mathbf{W}}(\mathbf{x}))=\text{Softmax}(\mathbf{f}^{\mathbf{W}}(\mathbf{x})).
paras.zh: 对于回归任务，我们通常将似然定义为高斯分布，其均值由模型输出给出：p(𝐲|𝐟^𝐖(𝐱))=𝒩(𝐟^𝐖(𝐱), σ²)，其中σ为观测噪声标量。而对于分类，我们通常将模型输出通过softmax函数压缩，并从得到的概率向量中采样：p(𝐲|𝐟^𝐖(𝐱))=Softmax(𝐟^𝐖(𝐱))。

paras.en: BNNs are easy to formulate, but difficult to perform inference in. This is because the marginal probability p​(𝐘|𝐗)𝑝conditional𝐘𝐗p(\mathbf{Y}|\mathbf{X}), required to evaluate the posterior p​(𝐖|𝐗,𝐘)=p​(𝐘|𝐗,𝐖)​p​(𝐖)/p​(𝐘|𝐗)𝑝conditional𝐖𝐗𝐘𝑝conditional𝐘𝐗𝐖𝑝𝐖𝑝conditional𝐘𝐗p(\mathbf{W}|\mathbf{X},\mathbf{Y})=p(\mathbf{Y}|\mathbf{X},\mathbf{W})p(\mathbf{W})/p(\mathbf{Y}|\mathbf{X}), cannot be evaluated analytically. Different approximations exist [12, 13, 14, 15]. In these approximate inference techniques, the posterior p​(𝐖|𝐗,𝐘)𝑝conditional𝐖𝐗𝐘p(\mathbf{W}|\mathbf{X},\mathbf{Y}) is fitted with a simple distribution qθ∗​(𝐖)superscriptsubscript𝑞𝜃𝐖q_{\theta}^{*}(\mathbf{W}), parameterised by θ𝜃\theta. This replaces the intractable problem of averaging over all weights in the BNN with an optimisation task, where we seek to optimise over the parameters of the simple distribution instead of optimising the original neural network’s parameters.
paras.zh: BNN易于构建，但难以进行推理。这是因为评估后验p(𝐖|𝐗,𝐘)=p(𝐘|𝐗,𝐖)p(𝐖)/p(𝐘|𝐗)所需的边缘概率p(𝐘|𝐗)无法解析计算。存在不同的近似方法[12, 13, 14, 15]。在这些近似推理技术中，后验p(𝐖|𝐗,𝐘)用一个由θ参数化的简单分布q_θ^*(𝐖)来拟合。这将BNN中所有权重平均的难解问题替换为一个优化任务，其中我们寻求优化简单分布的参数，而不是优化原始神经网络的参数。

paras.en: Dropout variational inference is a practical approach for approximate inference in large and complex models [15]. This inference is done by training a model with dropout before every weight layer, and by also performing dropout at test time to sample from the approximate posterior (stochastic forward passes, referred to as Monte Carlo dropout). More formally, this approach is equivalent to performing approximate variational inference where we find a simple distribution qθ∗​(𝐖)superscriptsubscript𝑞𝜃𝐖q_{\theta}^{*}(\mathbf{W}) in a tractable family which minimises the Kullback-Leibler (KL) divergence to the true model posterior p​(𝐖|𝐗,𝐘)𝑝conditional𝐖𝐗𝐘p(\mathbf{W}|\mathbf{X},\mathbf{Y}). Dropout can be interpreted as a variational Bayesian approximation, where the approximating distribution is a mixture of two Gaussians with small variances and the mean of one of the Gaussians is fixed at zero. The minimisation objective is given by [16]:
paras.zh: Dropout变分推理是一种在大规模和复杂模型中进行近似推理的实用方法[15]。这种推理通过在每层权重前使用dropout训练模型，并在测试时也进行dropout以从近似后验中采样（随机前向传播，称为蒙特卡洛dropout）来实现。更正式地说，该方法等价于执行近似变分推理，其中我们在一个易于处理的族中找到一个简单分布q_θ^*(𝐖)，该分布最小化与真实模型后验p(𝐖|𝐗,𝐘)的Kullback-Leibler（KL）散度。Dropout可以解释为一种变分贝叶斯近似，其中近似分布是两个具有小方差的高斯分布的混合，且其中一个高斯分布的均值固定为零。最小化目标由[16]给出：

paras.en: with N𝑁N data points, dropout probability p𝑝p, samples 𝐖^i∼qθ∗​(𝐖)similar-tosubscript^𝐖𝑖superscriptsubscript𝑞𝜃𝐖{\widehat{\mathbf{W}}}_{i}\sim q_{\theta}^{*}(\mathbf{W}), and θ𝜃\theta the set of the simple distribution’s parameters to be optimised (weight matrices in dropout’s case). In regression, for example, the negative log likelihood can be further simplified as
paras.zh: 其中N个数据点，dropout概率p，样本𝐖̂_i ∼ q_θ^*(𝐖)，θ是要优化的简单分布参数集（在dropout情况下为权重矩阵）。例如在回归中，负对数似然可以进一步简化为

paras.en: for a Gaussian likelihood, with σ𝜎\sigma the model’s observation noise parameter – capturing how much noise we have in the outputs.
paras.zh: 对于高斯似然，其中σ是模型的观测噪声参数——捕捉输出中的噪声量。

paras.en: Epistemic uncertainty in the weights can be reduced by observing more data. This uncertainty induces prediction uncertainty by marginalising over the (approximate) weights posterior distribution. For classification this can be approximated using Monte Carlo integration as follows:
paras.zh: 权重中的认知不确定性可以通过观测更多数据来减少。这种不确定性通过边缘化（近似）权重后验分布来引起预测不确定性。对于分类，这可以使用蒙特卡洛积分近似如下：

paras.en: with T𝑇T sampled masked model weights 𝐖^t∼qθ∗​(𝐖)similar-tosubscript^𝐖𝑡superscriptsubscript𝑞𝜃𝐖{\widehat{\mathbf{W}}}_{t}\sim q_{\theta}^{*}(\mathbf{W}), where qθ​(𝐖)subscript𝑞𝜃𝐖q_{\theta}(\mathbf{W}) is the Dropout distribution [6]. The uncertainty of this probability vector 𝐩𝐩\mathbf{p} can then be summarised using the entropy of the probability vector: H​(𝐩)=−∑c=1Cpc​log⁡pc.𝐻𝐩superscriptsubscript𝑐1𝐶subscript𝑝𝑐subscript𝑝𝑐H(\mathbf{p})=-\sum_{c=1}^{C}p_{c}\log p_{c}. For regression this epistemic uncertainty is captured by the predictive variance, which can be approximated as:
paras.zh: 其中T个采样掩码模型权重𝐖̂_t ∼ q_θ^*(𝐖)，q_θ(𝐖)是Dropout分布[6]。然后，该概率向量𝐩的不确定性可以使用概率向量的熵来概括：H(𝐩)=−∑_{c=1}^C p_c log p_c。对于回归，这种认知不确定性由预测方差捕捉，可以近似为：

paras.en: with predictions in this epistemic model done by approximating the predictive mean: E​(𝐲)≈1T​∑t=1T𝐟𝐖^t​(𝐱).𝐸𝐲1𝑇superscriptsubscript𝑡1𝑇superscript𝐟subscript^𝐖𝑡𝐱E(\mathbf{y})\approx\frac{1}{T}\sum_{t=1}^{T}\mathbf{f}^{{\widehat{\mathbf{W}}}_{t}}(\mathbf{x}). The first term in the predictive variance, σ2superscript𝜎2\sigma^{2}, corresponds to the amount of noise inherent in the data (which will be explained in more detail soon). The second part of the predictive variance measures how much the model is uncertain about its predictions – this term will vanish when we have zero parameter uncertainty (i.e. when all draws 𝐖^tsubscript^𝐖𝑡{\widehat{\mathbf{W}}}_{t} take the same constant value).
paras.zh: 其中该认知模型中的预测通过近似预测均值完成：E(𝐲)≈(1/T)∑_{t=1}^T 𝐟^{𝐖̂_t}(𝐱)。预测方差的第一项σ²对应于数据中固有的噪声量（稍后将详细解释）。预测方差的第二部分衡量模型对其预测的不确定性程度——当参数不确定性为零时（即所有抽取的𝐖̂_t取相同的常数值），该项将消失。

paras.en: In the above we captured model uncertainty – uncertainty over the model parameters – by approximating the distribution p​(𝐖|𝐗,𝐘)𝑝conditional𝐖𝐗𝐘p(\mathbf{W}|\mathbf{X},\mathbf{Y}). To capture aleatoric uncertainty in regression, we would have to tune the observation noise parameter σ𝜎\sigma.
paras.zh: 上述内容通过近似分布 p(𝐖|𝐗,𝐘) 捕捉了模型不确定性——即模型参数的不确定性。为了在回归中捕捉偶然不确定性，我们需要调整观测噪声参数 σ。

paras.en: Homoscedastic regression assumes constant observation noise σ𝜎\sigma for every input point 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x}. Heteroscedastic regression, on the other hand, assumes that observation noise can vary with input 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x} [17, 18]. Heteroscedastic models are useful in cases where parts of the observation space might have higher noise levels than others. In non-Bayesian neural networks, this observation noise parameter is often fixed as part of the model’s weight decay, and ignored. However, when made data-dependent, it can be learned as a function of the data:
paras.zh: 同方差回归假设每个输入点 𝐱 的观测噪声 σ 是常数。而异方差回归则假设观测噪声随输入 𝐱 变化 [17, 18]。当观测空间的某些部分噪声水平高于其他部分时，异方差模型非常有用。在非贝叶斯神经网络中，这个观测噪声参数通常作为模型权重衰减的一部分被固定并忽略。然而，当使其依赖于数据时，它可以作为数据的函数被学习：

paras.en: with added weight decay parameterised by λ𝜆\lambda (and similarly for l1subscript𝑙1l_{1} loss). Note that here, unlike the above, variational inference is not performed over the weights, but instead we perform MAP inference – finding a single value for the model parameters θ𝜃\theta. This approach does not capture epistemic model uncertainty, as epistemic uncertainty is a property of the model and not of the data.
paras.zh: 其中添加了由 λ 参数化的权重衰减（对于 l1 损失类似）。注意，与上述不同，这里不对权重进行变分推断，而是执行 MAP 推断——为模型参数 θ 寻找单一值。这种方法不捕捉认知模型不确定性，因为认知不确定性是模型的属性，而非数据的属性。

paras.en: In the next section we will combine these two types of uncertainties together in a single model. We will see how heteroscedastic noise can be interpreted as model attenuation, and develop a complimentary approach for the classification case.
paras.zh: 在下一节中，我们将把这两种类型的不确定性结合到同一个模型中。我们将看到异方差噪声如何被解释为模型衰减，并为分类情况开发一种补充方法。

full.sec: 3 Combining Aleatoric and Epistemic Uncertainty in One Model
full.secZh: 3 在同一模型中结合偶然不确定性和认知不确定性

paras.en: In the previous section we described existing Bayesian deep learning techniques. In this section we present novel contributions which extend this existing literature. We develop models that will allow us to study the effects of modeling either aleatoric uncertainty alone, epistemic uncertainty alone, or modeling both uncertainties together in a single model. This is followed by an observation that aleatoric uncertainty in regression tasks can be interpreted as learned loss attenuation – making the loss more robust to noisy data. We follow that by extending the ideas of heteroscedastic regression to classification tasks. This allows us to learn loss attenuation for classification tasks as well.
paras.zh: 在上一节中，我们描述了现有的贝叶斯深度学习技术。在本节中，我们提出了一些新的贡献，以扩展这些现有文献。我们开发了一些模型，使我们能够研究仅建模偶然不确定性、仅建模认知不确定性，或在同一模型中同时建模这两种不确定性的效果。随后，我们观察到回归任务中的偶然不确定性可以解释为学习到的损失衰减——使损失对噪声数据更加鲁棒。接着，我们将异方差回归的思想扩展到分类任务。这也使我们能够为分类任务学习损失衰减。

full.sec: 3.1 Combining Heteroscedastic Aleatoric Uncertainty and Epistemic Uncertainty
full.secZh: 3.1 结合异方差偶然不确定性和认知不确定性

paras.en: We wish to capture both epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty in a vision model. For this we turn the heteroscedastic NN in §2.2 into a Bayesian NN by placing a distribution over its weights, with our construction in this section developed specifically for the case of vision models111Although this construction can be generalised for any heteroscedastic NN architecture..
paras.zh: 我们希望在一个视觉模型中同时捕捉认知不确定性和偶然不确定性。为此，我们将第2.2节中的异方差神经网络转化为贝叶斯神经网络，通过在其权重上放置一个分布来实现，本节中的构造专门针对视觉模型的情况开发。

paras.en: We need to infer the posterior distribution for a BNN model 𝐟𝐟\mathbf{f} mapping an input image, 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x}, to a unary output, 𝐲^∈ℝ^𝐲ℝ\hat{\mathbf{y}}\in\mathbb{R}, and a measure of aleatoric uncertainty given by variance, σ2superscript𝜎2\mathbf{\sigma}^{2}. We approximate the posterior over the BNN with a dropout variational distribution using the tools of §2.1. As before, we draw model weights from the approximate posterior 𝐖^∼q​(𝐖)similar-to^𝐖𝑞𝐖{\widehat{\mathbf{W}}}\sim q(\mathbf{W}) to obtain a model output, this time composed of both predictive mean as well as predictive variance:
paras.zh: 我们需要推断一个BNN模型的后验分布，该模型将输入图像x映射到一元输出ŷ∈ℝ和由方差σ²给出的偶然不确定性度量。我们使用第2.1节的工具，通过dropout变分分布来近似BNN的后验。如前所述，我们从近似后验Ŵ∼q(W)中抽取模型权重以获得模型输出，这次输出包括预测均值和预测方差：

paras.en: where 𝐟𝐟\mathbf{f} is a Bayesian convolutional neural network parametrised by model weights 𝐖^^𝐖{\widehat{\mathbf{W}}}. We can use a single network to transform the input 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x}, with its head split to predict both 𝐲^^𝐲\hat{\mathbf{y}} as well as σ^2superscript^𝜎2\hat{\sigma}^{2}.
paras.zh: 其中f是一个贝叶斯卷积神经网络，由模型权重Ŵ参数化。我们可以使用单个网络来变换输入x，其头部被分割以同时预测ŷ和σ̂²。

paras.en: We fix a Gaussian likelihood to model our aleatoric uncertainty. This induces a minimisation objective given labeled output points x𝑥x:
paras.zh: 我们固定一个高斯似然来建模我们的偶然不确定性。这引出了一个给定标记输出点x的最小化目标：

paras.en: where D𝐷D is the number of output pixels 𝐲isubscript𝐲𝑖\mathbf{y}_{i} corresponding to input image 𝐱𝐱\mathbf{x}, indexed by i (additionally, the loss includes weight decay which is omitted for brevity). For example, we may set D=1𝐷1D=1 for image-level regression tasks, or D𝐷D equal to the number of pixels for dense prediction tasks (predicting a unary corresponding to each input image pixel). σ^i2subscriptsuperscript^𝜎2𝑖\hat{\sigma}^{2}_{i} is the BNN output for the predicted variance for pixel i𝑖i.
paras.zh: 其中D是对应于输入图像x的输出像素yi的数量，由i索引（此外，损失包括权重衰减，为简洁起见省略）。例如，对于图像级回归任务，我们可以设置D=1，或者对于密集预测任务（预测对应于每个输入图像像素的一元输出），D等于像素数。σ̂²_i是像素i的预测方差的BNN输出。

paras.en: This loss consists of two components; the residual regression obtained with a stochastic sample through the model – making use of the uncertainty over the parameters – and an uncertainty regularization term. We do not need ‘uncertainty labels’ to learn uncertainty. Rather, we only need to supervise the learning of the regression task. We learn the variance, σ2superscript𝜎2\sigma^{2}, implicitly from the loss function. The second regularization term prevents the network from predicting infinite uncertainty (and therefore zero loss) for all data points.
paras.zh: 这个损失由两部分组成：通过模型的随机样本获得的残差回归——利用了参数的不确定性——以及一个不确定性正则化项。我们不需要‘不确定性标签’来学习不确定性。相反，我们只需要监督回归任务的学习。我们从损失函数中隐式地学习方差σ²。第二个正则化项防止网络对所有数据点预测无限大的不确定性（从而得到零损失）。

paras.en: In practice, we train the network to predict the log variance, si:=log⁡σ^i2assignsubscript𝑠𝑖superscriptsubscript^𝜎𝑖2s_{i}:=\log\hat{\sigma}_{i}^{2}:
paras.zh: 在实践中，我们训练网络预测对数方差si:=log σ̂²_i：

paras.en: This is because it is more numerically stable than regressing the variance, σ2superscript𝜎2\sigma^{2}, as the loss avoids a potential division by zero. The exponential mapping also allows us to regress unconstrained scalar values, where exp⁡(−si)subscript𝑠𝑖\exp(-s_{i}) is resolved to the positive domain giving valid values for variance.
paras.zh: 这是因为它在数值上比回归方差σ²更稳定，因为损失避免了潜在的除以零。指数映射还允许我们回归无约束的标量值，其中exp(-si)被解析为正域，为方差提供有效值。

paras.en: To summarize, the predictive uncertainty for pixel 𝐲𝐲\mathbf{y} in this combined model can be approximated using:
paras.zh: 总结来说，在这个组合模型中，像素y的预测不确定性可以近似为：

paras.en: with {𝐲^t,σ^t2}t=1Tsuperscriptsubscriptsubscript^𝐲𝑡superscriptsubscript^𝜎𝑡2𝑡1𝑇\{\hat{\mathbf{y}}_{t},\hat{\sigma}_{t}^{2}\}_{t=1}^{T} a set of T𝑇T sampled outputs: 𝐲^t,σ^t2=𝐟𝐖^t​(𝐱)subscript^𝐲𝑡superscriptsubscript^𝜎𝑡2superscript𝐟subscript^𝐖𝑡𝐱\hat{\mathbf{y}}_{t},\hat{\sigma}_{t}^{2}=\mathbf{f}^{{\widehat{\mathbf{W}}}_{t}}(\mathbf{x}) for randomly masked weights 𝐖^t∼q​(𝐖)similar-tosubscript^𝐖𝑡𝑞𝐖{\widehat{\mathbf{W}}}_{t}\sim q(\mathbf{W}).
paras.zh: 其中{ŷ_t, σ̂_t²}_{t=1}^T是T个采样输出的集合：ŷ_t, σ̂_t² = f^{Ŵ_t}(x)，其中Ŵ_t∼q(W)是随机掩码的权重。

full.sec: 3.2 Heteroscedastic Uncertainty as Learned Loss Attenuation
full.secZh: 3.2 异方差不确定性作为学习得到的损失衰减

paras.en: We observe that allowing the network to predict uncertainty, allows it effectively to temper the residual loss by exp⁡(−si)subscript𝑠𝑖\exp(-s_{i}), which depends on the data. This acts similarly to an intelligent robust regression function. It allows the network to adapt the residual’s weighting, and even allows the network to learn to attenuate the effect from erroneous labels. This makes the model more robust to noisy data: inputs for which the model learned to predict high uncertainty will have a smaller effect on the loss.
paras.zh: 我们观察到，允许网络预测不确定性，使其能够根据数据有效地通过 exp(-s_i) 来缓和残差损失。这类似于一种智能的鲁棒回归函数。它允许网络调整残差的权重，甚至使网络学会减弱错误标签的影响。这使得模型对噪声数据更加鲁棒：模型学会预测高不确定性的输入将对损失产生较小的影响。

paras.en: The model is discouraged from predicting high uncertainty for all points – in effect ignoring the data – through the log⁡σ2superscript𝜎2\log\sigma^{2} term. Large uncertainty increases the contribution of this term, and in turn penalizes the model: The model can learn to ignore the data – but is penalised for that. The model is also discouraged from predicting very low uncertainty for points with high residual error, as low σ2superscript𝜎2\sigma^{2} will exaggerate the contribution of the residual and will penalize the model. It is important to stress that this learned attenuation is not an ad-hoc construction, but a consequence of the probabilistic interpretation of the model.
paras.zh: 通过 log σ² 项，模型被阻止对所有点预测高不确定性——实际上就是忽略数据。大的不确定性会增加该项的贡献，进而惩罚模型：模型可以学会忽略数据——但会因此受到惩罚。模型也被阻止对残差误差高的点预测非常低的不确定性，因为低的 σ² 会放大残差的贡献并惩罚模型。需要强调的是，这种学习得到的衰减并非临时构造，而是模型概率解释的结果。

full.secZh: 3.3 分类任务中的异方差不确定性

paras.en: This learned loss attenuation property of heteroscedastic NNs in regression is a desirable effect for classification models as well. However, heteroscedastic NNs in classification are peculiar models because technically any classification task has input-dependent uncertainty. Nevertheless, the ideas above can be extended from regression heteroscedastic NNs to classification heteroscedastic NNs.
paras.zh: 这种在回归中异方差神经网络学到的损失衰减特性对于分类模型来说也是一种理想的特性。然而，分类中的异方差神经网络是特殊的模型，因为从技术上讲，任何分类任务都具有输入相关的不确定性。尽管如此，上述思想可以从回归异方差神经网络扩展到分类异方差神经网络。

paras.en: For this we adapt the standard classification model to marginalise over intermediate heteroscedastic regression uncertainty placed over the logit space. We therefore explicitly refer to our proposed model adaptation as a heteroscedastic classification NN.
paras.zh: 为此，我们调整标准分类模型，以边缘化放置在logit空间上的中间异方差回归不确定性。因此，我们明确地将我们提出的模型调整称为异方差分类神经网络。

paras.en: For classification tasks our NN predicts a vector of unaries 𝐟isubscript𝐟𝑖\mathbf{f}_{i} for each pixel i𝑖i, which when passed through a softmax operation, forms a probability vector 𝐩isubscript𝐩𝑖\mathbf{p}_{i}. We change the model by placing a Gaussian distribution over the unaries vector:
paras.zh: 对于分类任务，我们的神经网络为每个像素i预测一个一元向量f_i，该向量经过softmax操作后形成概率向量p_i。我们通过在一元向量上放置高斯分布来改变模型：

paras.en: Here 𝐟i𝐖,σi𝐖subscriptsuperscript𝐟𝐖𝑖superscriptsubscript𝜎𝑖𝐖\mathbf{f}^{\mathbf{W}}_{i},\sigma_{i}^{\mathbf{W}} are the network outputs with parameters 𝐖𝐖\mathbf{W}. This vector 𝐟i𝐖subscriptsuperscript𝐟𝐖𝑖\mathbf{f}^{\mathbf{W}}_{i} is corrupted with Gaussian noise with variance (σi𝐖)2superscriptsuperscriptsubscript𝜎𝑖𝐖2(\sigma_{i}^{\mathbf{W}})^{2} (a diagonal matrix with one element for each logit value), and the corrupted vector is then squashed with the softmax function to obtain 𝐩isubscript𝐩𝑖\mathbf{p}_{i}, the probability vector for pixel i𝑖i.
paras.zh: 这里f_i^W, σ_i^W是网络输出，参数为W。向量f_i^W被方差为(σ_i^W)^2的高斯噪声污染（一个对角矩阵，每个logit值对应一个元素），然后污染后的向量通过softmax函数压缩得到p_i，即像素i的概率向量。

paras.en: Our expected log likelihood for this model is given by:
paras.zh: 该模型的期望对数似然由下式给出：

paras.en: with c𝑐c the observed class for input i𝑖i, which gives us our loss function. Ideally, we would want to analytically integrate out this Gaussian distribution, but no analytic solution is known. We therefore approximate the objective through Monte Carlo integration, and sample unaries through the softmax function. We note that this operation is extremely fast because we perform the computation once (passing inputs through the model to get logits). We only need to sample from the logits, which is a fraction of the network’s compute, and therefore does not significantly increase the model’s test time. We can rewrite the above and obtain the following numerically-stable stochastic loss:
paras.zh: 其中c是输入i的观测类别，这给出了我们的损失函数。理想情况下，我们希望解析地积分掉这个高斯分布，但已知没有解析解。因此，我们通过蒙特卡洛积分来近似目标，并通过softmax函数对一元向量进行采样。我们注意到，这个操作非常快，因为我们只执行一次计算（将输入通过模型得到logits）。我们只需要从logits中采样，这仅占网络计算的一小部分，因此不会显著增加模型的测试时间。我们可以重写上述内容，得到以下数值稳定的随机损失：

paras.en: with xi,t,c′subscript𝑥𝑖𝑡superscript𝑐′x_{i,t,c^{\prime}} the c′superscript𝑐′c^{\prime} element in the logit vector 𝐱i,tsubscript𝐱𝑖𝑡\mathbf{x}_{i,t}.
paras.zh: 其中x_{i,t,c'}是logit向量x_{i,t}的第c'个元素。

paras.en: This objective can be interpreted as learning loss attenuation, similarly to the regression case. We next assess the ideas above empirically.
paras.zh: 这个目标可以解释为学习损失衰减，类似于回归情况。接下来，我们通过实验评估上述想法。

paras.en: In this section we evaluate our methods with pixel-wise depth regression and semantic segmentation. An analysis of these results is given in the following section. To show the robustness of our learned loss attenuation – a side-effect of modeling uncertainty – we present results on an array of popular datasets, CamVid, Make3D, and NYUv2 Depth, where we set new state-of-the-art benchmarks.
paras.zh: 在本节中，我们通过像素级深度回归和语义分割来评估我们的方法。下一节将对这些结果进行分析。为了展示我们学习到的损失衰减（不确定性建模的副产品）的鲁棒性，我们在多个流行数据集（CamVid、Make3D 和 NYUv2 Depth）上展示了结果，并在这些数据集上取得了新的最佳基准。

paras.en: For the following experiments we use the DenseNet architecture [19] which has been adapted for dense prediction tasks by [20]. We use our own independent implementation of the architecture using TensorFlow [21] (which slightly outperforms the original authors’ implementation on CamVid by 0.2%, see Table 1(a)). For all experiments we train with 224×224224224224\times 224 crops of batch size 4, and then fine-tune on full-size images with a batch size of 1. We train with RMS-Prop with a constant learning rate of 0.0010.0010.001 and weight decay 10−4superscript10410^{-4}.
paras.zh: 在以下实验中，我们使用 DenseNet 架构 [19]，该架构已被 [20] 改编用于密集预测任务。我们使用 TensorFlow [21] 独立实现了该架构（该实现在 CamVid 上比原作者实现略优 0.2%，见表 1(a)）。对于所有实验，我们使用批量大小为 4 的 224×224 裁剪进行训练，然后在批量大小为 1 的全尺寸图像上进行微调。我们使用 RMS-Prop 优化器，学习率恒定为 0.001，权重衰减为 10^{-4}。

paras.en: We compare the results of the Bayesian neural network models outlined in §3. We model epistemic uncertainty using Monte Carlo dropout (§2.1). The DenseNet architecture places dropout with p=0.2𝑝0.2p=0.2 after each convolutional layer. Following [22], we use 50 Monte Carlo dropout samples. We model aleatoric uncertainty with MAP inference using loss functions (8) and (12 in the appendix), for regression and classification respectively (§2.2). However, we derive the loss function using a Laplacian prior, as opposed to the Gaussian prior used for the derivations in §3. This is because it results in a loss function which applies a L1 distance on the residuals. Typically, we find this to outperform L2 loss for regression tasks in vision. We model the benefit of combining both epistemic uncertainty as well as aleatoric uncertainty using our developments presented in §3.
paras.zh: 我们比较了第 3 节中概述的贝叶斯神经网络模型的结果。我们使用 Monte Carlo dropout（第 2.1 节）对认知不确定性进行建模。DenseNet 架构在每个卷积层后放置 dropout，dropout 概率 p=0.2。遵循 [22]，我们使用 50 个 Monte Carlo dropout 样本。我们分别使用损失函数（8）和（附录中的 12）通过 MAP 推理对偶然不确定性进行建模，分别用于回归和分类（第 2.2 节）。然而，我们使用拉普拉斯先验推导损失函数，而不是第 3 节推导中使用的高斯先验。这是因为拉普拉斯先验得到的损失函数对残差应用 L1 距离。通常，我们发现这在视觉回归任务中优于 L2 损失。我们使用第 3 节中提出的方法，对结合认知不确定性和偶然不确定性的好处进行了建模。

paras.en: To demonstrate our method for semantic segmentation, we use two datasets, CamVid [8] and NYU v2 [23]. CamVid is a road scene understanding dataset with 367 training images and 233 test images, of day and dusk scenes, with 111111 classes. We resize images to 360×480360480360\times 480 pixels for training and evaluation. In Table 1(a) we present results for our architecture. Our method sets a new state-of-the-art on this dataset with mean intersection over union (IoU) score of 67.5%percent67.567.5\%. We observe that modeling both aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty improves over the baseline result. The implicit attenuation obtained from the aleatoric loss provides a larger improvement than the epistemic uncertainty model. However, the combination of both uncertainties improves performance even further. This shows that for this application it is more important to model aleatoric uncertainty, suggesting that epistemic uncertainty can be mostly explained away in this large data setting.
paras.zh: 为展示我们的语义分割方法，我们使用了两个数据集：CamVid [8] 和 NYU v2 [23]。CamVid 是一个道路场景理解数据集，包含 367 张训练图像和 233 张测试图像，涵盖白天和黄昏场景，共 11 个类别。我们将图像调整为 360×480 像素用于训练和评估。表 1(a) 展示了我们架构的结果。我们的方法在该数据集上取得了新的最优结果，平均交并比（IoU）得分为 67.5%。我们观察到，同时建模偶然不确定性和认知不确定性相比基线结果有所提升。从偶然损失中获得的隐式衰减比认知不确定性模型带来了更大的改进。然而，两种不确定性的结合进一步提升了性能。这表明对于该应用，建模偶然不确定性更为重要，暗示在此大数据设置下，认知不确定性大多可以被解释掉。

paras.en: Secondly, NYUv2 [23] is a challenging indoor segmentation dataset with 40 different semantic classes. It has 1449 images with resolution 640×480640480640\times 480 from 464 different indoor scenes. Table 1(b) shows our results. This dataset is much harder than CamVid because there is significantly less structure in indoor scenes compared to street scenes, and because of the increased number of semantic classes. We use DeepLabLargeFOV [24] as our baseline model. We observe a similar result (qualitative results given in Figure 6); we improve baseline performance by giving the model flexibility to estimate uncertainty and attenuate the loss. The effect is more pronounced, perhaps because the dataset is more difficult.
paras.zh: 其次，NYUv2 [23] 是一个具有挑战性的室内分割数据集，包含 40 个不同的语义类别。它拥有来自 464 个不同室内场景的 1449 张图像，分辨率为 640×480。表 1(b) 展示了我们的结果。该数据集比 CamVid 困难得多，因为室内场景相比街道场景结构显著较少，且语义类别数量增加。我们使用 DeepLabLargeFOV [24] 作为基线模型。我们观察到了类似的结果（定性结果见图 6）；通过赋予模型估计不确定性并衰减损失的灵活性，我们提升了基线性能。效果更为显著，可能是因为该数据集更困难。

paras.en: We demonstrate the efficacy of our method for regression using two popular monocular depth regression datasets, Make3D [25] and NYUv2 Depth [23]. The Make3D dataset consists of 400 training and 134 testing images, gathered using a 3-D laser scanner. We evaluate our method using the same standard as [26], resizing images to 345×460345460345\times 460 pixels and evaluating on pixels with depth less than 70​m70𝑚70m. NYUv2 Depth is taken from the same dataset used for classification above. It contains RGB-D imagery from 464 different indoor scenes. We compare to previous approaches for Make3D in Table 2(a) and NYUv2 Depth in Table 2(b), using standard metrics (for a description of these metrics please see [27]).
paras.zh: 我们使用两个流行的单目深度回归数据集 Make3D [25] 和 NYUv2 Depth [23] 展示了我们的方法在回归任务上的有效性。Make3D 数据集包含 400 张训练图像和 134 张测试图像，使用 3D 激光扫描仪收集。我们按照与 [26] 相同的标准评估方法，将图像调整为 345×460 像素，并评估深度小于 70m 的像素。NYUv2 Depth 来自上述分类所用的同一数据集，包含来自 464 个不同室内场景的 RGB-D 图像。我们在表 2(a) 和表 2(b) 中分别与 Make3D 和 NYUv2 Depth 的先前方法进行了比较，使用了标准指标（有关这些指标的描述，请参见 [27]）。

paras.en: These results show that aleatoric uncertainty is able to capture many aspects of this task which are inherently difficult. For example, in the qualitative results in Figure 6 and 6 we observe that aleatoric uncertainty is greater for large depths, reflective surfaces and occlusion boundaries in the image. These are common failure modes of monocular depth algorithms [26]. On the other hand, these qualitative results show that epistemic uncertainty captures difficulties due to lack of data. For example, we observe larger uncertainty for objects which are rare in the training set such as humans in the third example of Figure 6.
paras.zh: 这些结果表明，偶然不确定性能够捕捉该任务中许多固有困难的方面。例如，在图 6 的定性结果中，我们观察到对于大深度、反射表面和图像中的遮挡边界，偶然不确定性更大。这些是单目深度算法的常见失败模式 [26]。另一方面，这些定性结果表明，认知不确定性捕捉了由于数据不足而导致的困难。例如，我们观察到对于训练集中罕见的物体（如图 6 第三个示例中的人类），不确定性更大。

paras.en: In summary, we have demonstrated that our model can improve performance over non-Bayesian baselines by implicitly learning attenuation of systematic noise and difficult concepts. For example we observe high aleatoric uncertainty for distant objects and on object and occlusion boundaries.
paras.zh: 总之，我们已经证明，我们的模型可以通过隐式学习系统噪声和困难概念的衰减来提高非贝叶斯基线的性能。例如，我们观察到对于远处物体以及物体和遮挡边界，偶然不确定性较高。

full.sec: 5 Analysis: What Do Aleatoric and Epistemic Uncertainties Capture?
full.secZh: 5 分析：偶然不确定性和认知不确定性捕捉了什么？

paras.en: In §4 we showed that modeling aleatoric and epistemic uncertainties improves prediction performance, with the combination performing even better. In this section we wish to study the effectiveness of modeling aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty. In particular, we wish to quantify the performance of these uncertainty measurements and analyze what they capture.
paras.zh: 在第4节中，我们展示了建模偶然不确定性和认知不确定性能够提升预测性能，且两者结合效果更佳。在本节中，我们希望研究建模偶然不确定性和认知不确定性的有效性。具体而言，我们希望量化这些不确定性测量的性能，并分析它们捕捉了什么。

paras.en: Firstly, in Figure 2 we show precision-recall curves for regression and classification models. They show how our model performance improves by removing pixels with uncertainty larger than various percentile thresholds. This illustrates two behaviors of aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty measures. Firstly, it shows that the uncertainty measurements are able to correlate well with accuracy, because all curves are strictly decreasing functions. We observe that precision is lower when we have more points that the model is not certain about. Secondly, the curves for epistemic and aleatoric uncertainty models are very similar. This shows that each uncertainty ranks pixel confidence similarly to the other uncertainty, in the absence of the other uncertainty. This suggests that when only one uncertainty is explicitly modeled, it attempts to compensate for the lack of the alternative uncertainty when possible.
paras.zh: 首先，在图2中，我们展示了回归和分类模型的精确率-召回率曲线。这些曲线显示了通过移除不确定性大于不同百分位数阈值的像素，模型性能如何提升。这说明了偶然不确定性和认知不确定性度量的两种行为。首先，它表明不确定性度量能够与准确率良好相关，因为所有曲线都是严格递减函数。我们观察到，当模型不确定的点更多时，精确率更低。其次，认知和偶然不确定性模型的曲线非常相似。这表明，在缺乏另一种不确定性的情况下，每种不确定性对像素置信度的排序方式相似。这暗示当仅显式建模一种不确定性时，它会尽可能补偿另一种不确定性的缺失。

paras.en: Secondly, in Figure 3 we analyze the quality of our uncertainty measurement using calibration plots from our model on the test set. To form calibration plots for classification models, we discretize our model’s predicted probabilities into a number of bins, for all classes and all pixels in the test set. We then plot the frequency of correctly predicted labels for each bin of probability values. Better performing uncertainty estimates should correlate more accurately with the line y=x𝑦𝑥y=x in the calibration plots. For regression models, we can form calibration plots by comparing the frequency of residuals lying within varying thresholds of the predicted distribution. Figure 3 shows the calibration of our classification and regression uncertainties.
paras.zh: 其次，在图3中，我们使用模型在测试集上的校准图分析了不确定性度量的质量。为了形成分类模型的校准图，我们将模型预测的概率离散化为多个区间，涵盖测试集中所有类别和所有像素。然后，我们绘制每个概率区间内正确预测标签的频率。性能更好的不确定性估计应与校准图中的y=x线更准确地相关。对于回归模型，我们可以通过比较残差落在预测分布不同阈值内的频率来形成校准图。图3展示了我们的分类和回归不确定性的校准结果。

full.sec: 5.2 Uncertainty with Distance from Training Data
full.secZh: 5.2 与训练数据距离的不确定性

paras.en: Aleatoric uncertainty cannot be explained away with more data,
paras.zh: 偶然不确定性无法通过更多数据来解释消除。

paras.en: Aleatoric uncertainty does not increase for out-of-data examples (situations different from training set), whereas epistemic uncertainty does.
paras.zh: 偶然不确定性不会因超出数据样本（与训练集不同的情况）而增加，而认知不确定性则会。

paras.en: In Table 3 we give accuracy and uncertainty for models trained on increasing sized subsets of datasets. This shows that epistemic uncertainty decreases as the training dataset gets larger. It also shows that aleatoric uncertainty remains relatively constant and cannot be explained away with more data. Testing the models with a different test set (bottom two lines) shows that epistemic uncertainty increases considerably on those test points which lie far from the training sets.
paras.zh: 在表3中，我们给出了在数据集递增子集上训练的模型的准确性和不确定性。这表明，随着训练数据集的增大，认知不确定性会降低。同时，偶然不确定性保持相对恒定，无法通过更多数据来解释消除。使用不同的测试集（底部两行）测试模型表明，对于那些远离训练集的测试点，认知不确定性显著增加。

paras.en: These results reinforce the case that epistemic uncertainty can be explained away with enough data, but is required to capture situations not encountered in the training set. This is particularly important for safety-critical systems, where epistemic uncertainty is required to detect situations which have never been seen by the model before.
paras.zh: 这些结果进一步证实了认知不确定性可以通过足够的数据来解释消除，但对于捕捉训练集中未遇到的情况是必要的。这对于安全关键系统尤为重要，因为认知不确定性对于检测模型从未见过的情况是必需的。

paras.en: Our model based on DenseNet [20] can process a 640×480640480640\times 480 resolution image in 150​m​s150𝑚𝑠150ms on a NVIDIA Titan X GPU. The aleatoric uncertainty models add negligible compute. However, epistemic models require expensive Monte Carlo dropout sampling. For models such as ResNet [4], this is possible to achieve economically because only the last few layers contain dropout. Other models, like DenseNet, require the entire architecture to be sampled. This is difficult to parallelize due to GPU memory constraints, and often results in a 50×50\times slow-down for 50 Monte Carlo samples.
paras.zh: 我们基于 DenseNet [20] 的模型可以在 NVIDIA Titan X GPU 上以 150 毫秒处理一张 640×480 分辨率的图像。偶然不确定性模型增加的算力可忽略不计。然而，认知模型需要昂贵的蒙特卡洛 Dropout 采样。对于像 ResNet [4] 这样的模型，由于只有最后几层包含 Dropout，因此可以经济地实现这一点。其他模型，如 DenseNet，需要对整个架构进行采样。由于 GPU 内存限制，这难以并行化，并且对于 50 个蒙特卡洛样本，通常会导致 50 倍的减速。

paras.en: We presented a novel Bayesian deep learning framework to learn a mapping to aleatoric uncertainty from the input data, which is composed on top of epistemic uncertainty models. We derived our framework for both regression and classification applications. We showed that it is important to model aleatoric uncertainty for:
paras.zh: 我们提出了一种新颖的贝叶斯深度学习框架，用于从输入数据中学习到偶然不确定性的映射，该映射建立在认知不确定性模型之上。我们针对回归和分类应用推导了该框架。我们展示了建模偶然不确定性的重要性：

paras.en: Large data situations, where epistemic uncertainty is explained away,
paras.zh: 在大数据情况下，认知不确定性被解释掉；

paras.en: Real-time applications, because we can form aleatoric models without expensive Monte Carlo samples.
paras.zh: 在实时应用中，因为我们可以无需昂贵的蒙特卡洛样本就能形成偶然模型。

paras.en: And epistemic uncertainty is important for:
paras.zh: 而认知不确定性对于以下情况很重要：

paras.en: Safety-critical applications, because epistemic uncertainty is required to understand examples which are different from training data,
paras.zh: 安全关键应用，因为需要认知不确定性来理解与训练数据不同的样本；

paras.en: Small datasets where the training data is sparse.
paras.zh: 小数据集，其中训练数据稀疏。

paras.en: However aleatoric and epistemic uncertainty models are not mutually exclusive. We showed that the combination is able to achieve new state-of-the-art results on depth regression and semantic segmentation benchmarks.
paras.zh: 然而，偶然不确定性和认知不确定性模型并非互斥。我们展示了它们的组合能够在深度回归和语义分割基准上取得新的最先进结果。

paras.en: The first paragraph in this paper posed two recent disasters which could have been averted by real-time Bayesian deep learning tools. Therefore, we leave finding a method for real-time epistemic uncertainty in deep learning as an important direction for future research.
paras.zh: 本文第一段提出了两个最近的灾难，这些灾难本可以通过实时贝叶斯深度学习工具避免。因此，我们将寻找一种用于深度学习中实时认知不确定性的方法作为未来研究的重要方向。
